options.text: Write social media posts for my bakery. Use food emojis, mention Portland, end with our tagline.

I need social media posts for my Portland bakery. Please make them warm and friendly, include food emojis, mention that we're located in Portland, and always end with our tagline. Can you write a few examples?

I run a bakery's Instagram account and need help writing posts that connect with our local community. Our audience responds well to visual, casual content (that's why we use food emojis), and we want to emphasize our local roots since Portland customers love supporting neighborhood businesses (hence mentioning our location). We always end with our tagline because it reinforces our brand identity. The warm tone is important because we want people to feel like they're hearing from a friend, not a corporate account. Can you write some sample posts that capture this approach?

responses.response_weak:
Here are some social media posts for your bakery:

🥐 Fresh pastries available now in Portland! Stop by today. [Your tagline]

🍰 New cake flavors this week in Portland! Come try them out. [Your tagline]

🍪 Cookies are fresh out of the oven in Portland! Grab some before they're gone. [Your tagline]
responses.response_medium:
Here are some warm and friendly posts for your Portland bakery:

Good morning, Portland! There is nothing like starting your day with a warm croissant and a smile. 🥐☀️ Come see us today and treat yourself — you deserve it! [Your tagline]

Happy Friday, friends! We baked a little something extra special this weekend just for you. 🎂🍰 Swing by our Portland shop and see what is waiting in the display case! [Your tagline]

Rainy day? We have got the cure. Fresh-baked cookies, a cozy vibe, and the best coffee in Portland. ☕🍪 Come warm up with us! [Your tagline]
responses.response_strong:
Here are some Instagram posts that capture that neighborhood-bakery-talking-to-friends voice:

---

**Post 1 (Morning energy):**
That moment when you walk in and the whole shop smells like fresh sourdough... yeah, that is happening right now on SE Division. 🍞✨ Our baker was here at 4am making sure your morning is a good one. Swing by before the loaves disappear (we see you, regulars who show up at 7am sharp 😄). Sweet dreams are baked fresh daily.

---

**Post 2 (Weather tie-in):**
Portland rain + our cinnamon rolls = the only morning equation that matters. 🌧️🤗 We just pulled a fresh batch and they are absolutely ridiculous today. Pair one with a locally roasted oat milk latte and honestly, good luck leaving. Sweet dreams are baked fresh daily.

---

**Post 3 (Community/market):**
Guess who is going to be at the Hawthorne Sunday Market this weekend? 🙋‍♀️🥨 We are bringing all the favorites plus a brand new marionberry danish that might just be our best creation yet (big claim, we know). Come find us, say hi, and grab something delicious — supporting local never tasted this good. 🫐 Sweet dreams are baked fresh daily.

---

**Post 4 (Behind the scenes):**
Real talk: our kitchen at 5am looks like a flour explosion happened and honestly we would not have it any other way. 🤣🥖 Every loaf, every pastry, every crumbly messy beautiful thing we make is done by hand right here in our Portland kitchen. No shortcuts, just love (and butter... a lot of butter). 🧈 Sweet dreams are baked fresh daily.

feedback.what_happened: The weak prompt treats the AI like a template-filler, so it produces generic, repetitive posts that technically check your boxes but feel robotic. The medium prompt adds warmth and asks for examples, which gets you more natural language and better variety. But the effective prompt explains the WHY behind each requirement — why emojis work for your audience, why Portland matters to locals, why the tagline reinforces branding. This context lets the AI understand your actual goals, so it creates posts that sound authentically conversational, include specific Portland details (SE Division, Hawthorne Market), and naturally weave in your requirements instead of forcing them.
feedback.principle_name: When you explain WHY you need something done a certain way, the AI can match your actual intent instead of just following surface-level rules.
feedback.tip: Next time you have formatting requirements, don't just list them — explain the purpose behind each one. Instead of 'use emojis,' try 'use emojis because our audience responds to visual content' or 'mention our location because locals love supporting neighborhood businesses.'
